pi_items.source: task.json description (line 5) and metadata field; confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 9) and export_result.sh (line 9); directory creation verified in setup_task.sh (line 11) and setup_librecad.sh (lines 60-62)

task.json description explicitly states 'For this 6m-wide L-shape, the ridges and valley will intersect at coordinate (3,3).' Mathematical verification confirms (3,3) is where the valley from (6,6) at 45 degrees (following line y=x) meets the hip from corner (0,0) (also following y=x). export_result.sh verifies connectivity to point (3,3) at lines 103-104.

task.json description explicitly states 'Draw the valley line from the inner corner (6,6) inward.' This point is also confirmed as one of the L-shaped wall corners. export_result.sh verifies the valley line from (6,6) to (3,3) at lines 99-100.

task.json description explicitly states 'Draw an L-shaped closed polygon with these coordinates (in order): (0,0) -> (12,0) -> (12,6) -> (6,6) -> (6,10) -> (0,10) -> (0,0).' These six corners define an L-shaped building outline with the interior corner at (6,6).

task.json description explicitly states 'Create 3 layers: WALLS (White/Color 7), ROOF_LINES (Cyan/Color 4), and NOTES (Yellow/Color 2).' verifier.py (lines 62-70) also checks for these exact layer names (case-insensitive).

privileged_info_summary: The task is to create a hip roof plan in LibreCAD for an L-shaped building defined by six corner points: (0,0), (12,0), (12,6), (6,6), (6,10), (0,10). The output file must be saved as hip_roof_plan.dxf in /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/. The drawing requires three layers (WALLS, ROOF_LINES, NOTES) with specific geometric elements: a valley line from the interior corner (6,6) inward at 45 degrees, hip lines from the five exterior corners at 45 degrees, ridge lines connecting these features, and all roof geometry lines meeting/intersecting at point (3,3). Text annotations 'VALLEY' and 'RIDGE' must be added to the drawing. This is a synthetic task with no external data source; the geometry is procedurally defined based on architectural hip roof principles applied to the specified L-shaped building dimensions.
